findings.description: The core Brand Accent Shape definition (rectangle with two selectively enlarged opposite corner radii, other two slight radius) is not represented by any rule. Only the diagonal-orientation-consistency sub-rule was extracted; the foundational shape/geometry rule is missing entirely.

payload.description: Brand Accent Shape is a rectangle with two selectively enlarged opposite corner radii; the remaining two corners keep a slight radius.

findings.description: Approved print radius pairs (1.25"&0.0625", 0.5"&0.0625", 0.4375"&0.0625") are concrete, measurable spec values with no corresponding rule; this content is dropped from clustering entirely.

payload.description: Approved radius pairs for print: 1.25in & 0.0625in (large), 0.5in & 0.0625in (banner), 0.4375in & 0.0625in (small callout).

findings.description: Unit 0003 contains two distinct facts: (a) email px radius conversions (120/6, 48/6, 42/6px) as spec values, and (b) the diagonal-orientation consistency requirement. Only the latter is captured by the existing rule; the px conversion values are lost. These should be split into a separate spec_value rule and linked/related to the diagonal-consistency rule.

new_rule.description: Email px radius conversions at 96dpi: large 120px & 6px, banner 48px & 6px, callout 42px & 6px, applied to opposite corner pairs.

findings.description: The explicit exception for charts/tables (uniform rounded corners on all four corners at 12px, overriding the asymmetric accent shape) is completely missing from the rule set. This is a hard override/exception relative to the core shape rule and must be captured as its own rule with an exception relation, not folded into the diagonal-consistency rule.

payload.description: Charts and tables must use uniform rounded corners on all four corners (12px, GENERAL), not the asymmetric accent shape.

findings.description: Source explicitly tags the px conversions as [GENERAL], implying a channel/scope governance facet (general vs. print vs. email-specific applicability). The existing rule carries no scope/channel facet, losing this governance distinction between print (approved pairs) and email/general (px conversions).

payload.note: Add channel/scope facet distinguishing GENERAL email guidance from print-specific approved radius pairs.
